an3.55:1.1: Then the brahmin Jāṇussoṇi approached the *Bhagavā*, and having approached, paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side. Seated to one side, the brahmin Jāṇussoṇi said this to the *Bhagavā*:
an3.55:1.2: "'*Nibbāna* visible in this very life, *nibbāna* visible in this very life' — so it is said, Master Gotama.
an3.55:1.3: In what way, Master Gotama, is *nibbāna* visible in this very life, immediate, inviting one to come and see, leading onward, to be personally experienced by the wise?"
an3.55:2.1: When one is inflamed by passion, brahmin — overcome by passion, with mind [*citta*] seized — one intends harm to oneself, one intends harm to others, one intends harm to both; and one experiences mental *dukkha* and grief.
an3.55:2.2: When passion is abandoned, one no longer intends harm to oneself, no longer intends harm to others, no longer intends harm to both; and one no longer experiences mental *dukkha* and grief.
an3.55:2.3: In this way, brahmin, *nibbāna* is visible in this very life.
an3.55:3.1: When one is corrupted by hatred, brahmin …
an3.55:3.2: When one is deluded, brahmin — overcome by delusion, with mind [*citta*] seized — one intends harm to oneself, one intends harm to others, one intends harm to both; and one experiences mental *dukkha* and grief.
an3.55:3.3: When delusion is abandoned, one no longer intends harm to oneself, no longer intends harm to others, no longer intends harm to both; and one no longer experiences mental *dukkha* and grief.
an3.55:3.4: In this way, brahmin, *nibbāna* is visible in this very life.
an3.55:4.1: When, brahmin, one experiences the complete destruction of passion, the complete destruction of hatred, the complete destruction of delusion —
an3.55:4.2: in this way, brahmin, *nibbāna* is visible in this very life, immediate, inviting one to come and see, leading onward, to be personally experienced by the wise."
an3.55:5.2: May Master Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge from this day forth for as long as life lasts."
